item.title: Daily Briefing - 2026-06-13
item.description:
**Morning Briefing**
**What Changed Since Yesterday** Global markets showed mixed performance. The S&P 500 rose by 0.5%, while the Nasdaq fell by 0.3%. Oil prices edged up 1.2%. Treasury yields remain steady. Inflation data is expected later this week.
**Venezuela Update** Negotiations between the Venezuelan government and opposition parties are ongoing. A potential agreement on humanitarian aid is on the table. International reactions have been cautious but supportive of dialogue.
**UK Response Signals** The UK government announced a review of its energy policy. There is growing concern over rising energy costs for consumers. Key figures within the government advocate for immediate support measures.
**Legal/Docket Watch (Epstein-Related)** None.
**What to Watch Today** Monitor the upcoming inflation report scheduled for release. Pay attention to any developments from the Venezuelan negotiations. Watch for additional statements from UK officials on energy policies. Keep an eye on commodity markets.
End of briefing.
